enter.speaker: narrator
enter.text: The figure crouches beside the altar, one hand flat against the stone. He does not turn when you approach. His robes are damp and torn at the hem, and his hair hangs lank and pale. When he finally speaks, his voice is steady — the voice of someone who stopped being afraid a long time ago.

veyrin-speaks.speaker: veyrin
veyrin-speaks.text: You should not have come here. But I suppose Brenna sent you, or the token did. It amounts to the same thing.

choices.text: The Hermit told me you were down here.
Has the seal weakened since I left?
You look worse. How long have you been like this?
I have the materials. Silver, shadow essence, wraith dust.
I have come to end your watch.

hermit-sent.speaker: veyrin
hermit-sent.text: The old man. Yes. He would know. He has watched the water long enough to feel when something passes beneath it.

confirm-identity.speaker: veyrin
confirm-identity.text: Scholar. Fool. Keeper, now. The titles accumulate. None of them help.

about-altar.speaker: veyrin
about-altar.text: This is a seal. The altar is the lock and I am learning to be the key. Something is imprisoned beneath it — old, patient, and aware. The walls down here are not walls. They are a cage.

choices.text: The Hollow King — was he part of this?

the-prisoner.speaker: veyrin
the-prisoner.text: I do not know its name. The inscriptions call it the Sleeper, or the Root, or simply It. What I do know: if the seal breaks, Ashenvale will be the first thing it touches. And it will not stop there.

hollow-king.speaker: veyrin
hollow-king.text: The Hollow King was a warden. One of the last. His mind broke under the weight of what he was keeping down. He forgot why he stood guard and became part of the problem. You did what had to be done.

cannot-leave.speaker: veyrin
cannot-leave.text: No. The altar needs a keeper — a mind pressed against the seal at all times. If I leave, the stone cracks. Slowly at first, then not slowly at all.

no-other-way.speaker: veyrin
no-other-way.text: There may be. I have not found it yet. The inscriptions speak of a deeper seal — something that could hold without a keeper. But I cannot search for it and hold the altar at the same time. That is a problem for another day, and another pair of hands.

message.speaker: veyrin
message.text: Tell Brenna this: the seal holds. For now. She will understand. She always understood more than she let on.

message-2.speaker: narrator
message-2.text: Veyrin turns back to the altar. His hand finds the stone again, and his eyes close. The chamber hums — a low, steady vibration you feel more than hear. It sounds like something breathing in its sleep.

farewell.speaker: veyrin
farewell.text: Go. The way up is still open. Do not linger — the things down here grow curious when they sense warmth.

veyrin-seal-update.speaker: veyrin
veyrin-seal-update.text: It has, in places. Not enough to break — but enough to teach me where the cracks are. The pages have grown louder since you began returning. The thing beneath turns its attention toward warmth, and you bring more of it each time you come down.

veyrin-seal-update-2.speaker: veyrin
veyrin-seal-update-2.text: Do not stop coming. The visits steady me. A keeper without a witness eventually becomes the thing he keeps. I have watched it happen, in the inscriptions. I would prefer not to be a footnote.

last-seal-offer.speaker: veyrin
last-seal-offer.text: Long enough. The seal has been drinking from me — slowly at first, then more. I cough out paper and wake with my hand still on the stone. I am not the man you found here. I am what is left after the man was poured into the lock.

last-seal-offer-2.speaker: veyrin
last-seal-offer-2.text: I am not asking for rescue. There is no rescue. There is only what shape the ending takes, and how soon. I will lay it plain to you, because plain is the only courtesy I have left in me.

last-seal-choice.speaker: veyrin
last-seal-choice.text: Path one. Reinforce the lock. The inscriptions name three things — silver from the deep ore, shadow essence from the wraiths beneath, wraith dust from the same. Bring them to me. I work them into the stone. I survive — weaker, but standing — and the seal holds for years.

last-seal-choice-2.speaker: veyrin
last-seal-choice-2.text: Path two. End me. End the keeper. The seal cracks the moment the breath leaves my body, and what is below begins its slow turning toward the surface. The world will not love you for it. I will. I will love you for the kindness of the choosing.

last-seal-choice-3.speaker: veyrin
last-seal-choice-3.text: I will not push you toward either. I have weighed both for thirty-one nights. The scales do not settle. Decide. Do not decide tonight if tonight is not the night for it. I will be here. I am always here.

last-seal-accept.speaker: narrator
last-seal-accept.text: He turns back to the altar. His hand finds the stone the way another man's hand finds a wife's shoulder in sleep — without looking, without thinking. The chamber hums. You take the stairs slowly.

last-seal-reinforce.speaker: narrator
last-seal-reinforce.text: Veyrin takes the silver first, and the wraith dust second, and the shadow essence last. He arranges them along the altar's seam — the small bright thing, the dim grey thing, the thing that drinks the light from your lantern.

last-seal-reinforce-2.speaker: narrator
last-seal-reinforce-2.text: He works in silence for a long time. The chamber's hum changes pitch — lower, slower, steadier. Veyrin sags against the altar when it is done, paler by half, but the shaking in his hands has stopped.

last-seal-reinforce-3.speaker: veyrin
last-seal-reinforce-3.text: It will hold. Years, perhaps. I will hold with it. I am the seal now in a way I was not yesterday, and there is no taking that back. Thank you. The rescue you did not bring was the better word for it.

last-seal-reinforce-4.speaker: veyrin
last-seal-reinforce-4.text: Six hundred gold from a coin-purse Brenna left with me three years ago. One thousand one hundred experience. The numbers are not balanced. Nothing here is balanced. Take them.

last-seal-reinforce-close.speaker: veyrin
last-seal-reinforce-close.text: Come back. Not for the seal. For the company. The thing beneath the stone is patient. So is loneliness. Both kill the keeper, given time. Only one of them lets a friend at the door.

last-seal-mercy.speaker: veyrin
last-seal-mercy.text: ...Yes. You see it the way I see it. There are no good answers. Only the answer that lets a man stop being the lock he was poured into. Sit with me a moment. Then do it cleanly. I have been clean about most things in my life. Let the last be no different.

last-seal-mercy-2.speaker: narrator
last-seal-mercy-2.text: He turns to face the altar one last time. He places both hands flat on the stone. He whispers something in a language that is not quite speech — the same sound the chamber has been making, only softer. Then he is still. You do what was asked. The chamber's hum falters and is gone.

last-seal-mercy-3.speaker: narrator
last-seal-mercy-3.text: The altar cracks once across the centre. Not loud. Not dramatic. A long thin line, like a hair laid on stone. From beneath it, far down, something turns its attention toward the warmth of the chamber, and pauses. Patient. Waiting.

last-seal-mercy-4.speaker: narrator
last-seal-mercy-4.text: On the altar, in coin-shape, eight hundred gold. The keeper had been preparing the payment for some time. The way up is still open. The way up is, for the moment, still open.
